tipRules.tip: Bank fishing tip: Insert a nail weight in the Senko's tail to make it enter the water like a dart and shoot under pad/mat cover — great during spring/summer rains when big fish move shallow
Bank fishing tip: For blind-casting to active baitfish activity, let Senko sink to mid-depth then use moderate retrieve speed with short rapid rod tip twitches for lots of motion
Bank fishing tip: Use a 4/0 straight shank worm hook for most applications — slips in and out of cover well, slides across matted grass. Go 5/0 for 6-inch, 3/0 for 3-inch
Bank fishing tip: Senko on shaky head is good for probing dock pilings, culvert edges, riprap points and bridge pilings
Bank fishing tip: A 3-inch Senko on Ned head is ideal where small bluegill abound — can also trim a 5-6 inch Senko to fit
Bank fishing tip: Neko rig for faster fall with bottom feeding presentation — optionally add VMC Neko Skirt for pizazz

knowledge.insight: Bank fishing: 'Shooting' technique from crappie fishing — point rod at target, pull bait toward you to load rod tip with downward arc, release like bow and arrow. Great for docks, laydowns, gaps in weeds

Adding accent color to Senko's tail can turn lookers into biters. Chartreuse mimics bream fin tips. Red and orange also work. Rubbing the tail vigorously lightens the plastic as a field-expedient color change

Adding a tail spinner blade to a Senko via Owner Flashy Spin on a centering pin is effective in darker water — swimming the bait through sunlit areas throws attention-getting flashes into shadow areas where predators lurk
For bank fishing Senkos: 7-foot medium-heavy spinning outfit. Use Tactical Anglers Power Clip to swap rigs without re-tying
